name: AgentOps Docs and Release Governance Canary
description: Offline public canary for documentation governance: doc-release stabilization, skill-count sync, CLI-skills map parity, hook/docs parity, CI policy parity, source-backed doc/readme contracts, and OSS docs audit behavior.

cases.title: doc-release stabilization gate passes
cases.objective: Baseline the full doc-release gate that validates links, skill counts, skill-count sync, CLI-skills map parity, and release message freeze.

cases.title: docs policy parity gates pass
cases.objective: Keep hook/docs parity, CI blocking policy parity, CLI-skills map generation count, and skill-count sync directly protected as eval canaries.

inputs.shell: bash scripts/validate-hooks-doc-parity.sh && bash scripts/validate-ci-policy-parity.sh && bash scripts/validate-cli-skills-map.sh && scripts/sync-skill-counts.sh --check

expectations.value: DONE: All counts already in sync.

cases.title: doc, README, and OSS docs skill validators pass across runtimes
cases.objective: Keep documentation authoring skills healthy across shared and Codex artifacts, including README validation and OSS documentation workflows.

inputs.shell: bash skills/doc/scripts/validate.sh && bash skills-codex/doc/scripts/validate.sh && bash skills/readme/scripts/validate.sh && bash skills-codex/readme/scripts/validate.sh && bash skills/oss-docs/scripts/validate.sh && bash skills-codex/oss-docs/scripts/validate.sh

cases.title: OSS docs audit emits valid tiered JSON
cases.objective: Behaviorally protect the OSS docs audit command's JSON mode so future doc tooling can consume project type, tier scores, and totals deterministically.

inputs.shell: skills/oss-docs/scripts/audit-oss-docs.sh --json | jq -e '.tier1.total == 4 and .tier2.total == 5 and .tier3.total == 6 and .total_possible == 15' >/dev/null && echo oss-docs-json-ok

cases.title: documentation and README skills preserve source-backed claims
cases.objective: Lock the docs/readme rules that prevent stale or marketing-heavy output: source-backed updates, claim validation, authoritative docs, and trust blocks.

expectations.value: Establish ONE authoritative file per domain. Other docs MUST reference, not duplicate.
Prefer source-backed updates, doc-gap findings, and validation commands over generic prose.
Validate claims against the repo before presenting the draft as ready.

cases.title: doc-release scripts preserve fail-closed parity contracts
cases.objective: Ensure documentation release scripts keep their fail-closed checks for release notes, skill counts, CLI mapping, hooks docs, and CI blocking policy.

expectations.value: top audit line must declare '<N> generated CLI command headings'
